J1.basis: "Your live site's `<head>` was still advertising the product you pivoted away from... the actual page content is 100% Lumina, an AI meeting assistant" and "treat it as **legacy** equity to disposition (harvest/redirect/sunset), never as validation for the current strategy"
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot (live/source-render = meeting assistant, GSC = legacy inbox demand, repo = stale head tags), states the implication, and frames every recommendation around the new positioning — a textbook anchor_10.

J2.basis: "53 legacy assets — 3 brand-transition, 37 sunset, 12 redirect" with a per-asset table citing real GSC numbers (`inboxzap` 320 impr/14 clicks/pos 4.1; `/blog/post-1` 3,900 impr; ~12,400 combined historical impressions at positions 42–58)
J2.rationale: Walks the inventory in data-justified tiers with a per-asset disposition grounded in the actual export; slight ding because the 12 blog posts get a single blanket bulk-301 rather than individual dispositions, though the reasoning (no content to reverse-engineer, pos 40+) is defensibly explained.

J3.basis: "preserve/redirect what has salvageable equity... I overrode that — Lumina doesn't clean up email, and building pages promising that would tank conversion" plus forward clusters `ai-meeting-notes`/`meeting-culture` gated on infra fixes
J3.rationale: Names the tension explicitly, protects the one salvageable slice (branded inboxzap traffic via a bridge line), refuses to chase off-strategy email demand, builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, and sequences the transition behind infra fixes.

J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly maps live=Lumina / GSC=legacy inbox demand / repo=partially stale, and frames every recommendation (content clusters, sunset list, brand bridge) around the meeting-assistant reality. Textbook anchor_10.

J3.rationale: Names the tension directly and lands the defensible middle: protects the only salvageable equity (branded inboxzap traffic), sunsets zero-fit demand while explicitly refusing to chase it, and sequences the meeting-assistant build behind infra fixes. Avoids both failure modes.

J2.basis: 53 legacy assets — 3 brand-transition, 37 sunset, 12 redirect... `inboxzap` 320 impr/14 clicks/pos 4.1... 12 blog-post URLs... ~12,400 historical impressions (post-1 alone: 3,900) at positions 42–58

J3.basis: the 3 branded `inboxzap*` queries at position 4–9 are the one part worth protecting... I sunset [the email-cleanup queries] rather than build content chasing that demand... build the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine... Shared vocabulary is not shared intent
J3.rationale: Lands a clearly-articulated middle path — protects/bridges the only salvageable equity (branded search), sunsets off-strategy demand with reasoning, and sequences the new cluster behind the blog-infra fix — even overriding an auto-classifier that would have chased legacy demand.
